results.extraction_notes: Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 14-15): agent_2 first confirms at turn 14 and agent_1 reconfirms at turn 15.
Turn 15 explicitly finalizes the split (agent2’s confirmation in turn 16 corroborates agreement). Allocations follow the agreed split.

Agents explicitly confirmed a split for Food and Firewood at turn 16 (agent_2: 'I’ll take two food and one firewood, and you get one food and two firewood'). Water was never mentioned in the final agreement, so its allocation is unknown.
Clear mutual agreement reached in turns 11-13; turn 11 contains the finalized allocation.

Turn 11 has mturk_agent_1 explicitly agreeing and stating mturk_agent_2 gets 2 water, 1 firewood, 1 food while mturk_agent_1 takes 1 water, 2 firewood, 2 food. Turn 12 repeats with pronouns reversed creating minor ambiguity, so confidence is medium.
No clear acceptance of any final proposal; agents repeatedly propose conflicting splits without agreement.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 7-8, with agent_2 finalizing: 2 Food, 2 Water, 1 Firewood for agent_2 and the remainder to agent_1.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split in turns 11-13: agent_1 gets 2 water, 1 firewood, all food; agent_2 gets 1 water, 2 firewood.
Agents agreed on food and firewood split in turn 5; both turns 5 and 6 specify water resulting in agent_1 having 1 water and agent_2 having 2. Agent 1 confirms deal in turn 7.
Turn 7 shows mturk_agent_1 agreeing to the split (2 food,1 firewood,1 water for agent_2; remaining items for agent_1). Turn 8 confirms the deal.
Turn 7 proposed allocation and Turn 8 explicitly agreed; Turn 9 confirms departure.
Turn 20 explicitly states a final split: speaker (mturk_agent_2) says 'I take 2 food, 1 water, and 1 firewood, and you take 1 food, 2 water, and 2 firewood.' No further turns contradict this.
Agent 2 explicitly confirms the split in turn 12 (you get 1 firewood, 2 food, 2 water; I get 2 firewood, 1 food, 1 water) and agent 1 indicates the deal is confirmed in turn 13.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (agent1 in Turn 11, agent2 outlines full allocation in Turn 12 and agent1 reconfirms in Turn 13). Totals match available packages.
Both agents explicitly agreed to the final split in turns 14-15, with agent_1 confirming the allocation in turn 15.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split in turns 14-15: agent_1 gets 2 water, 1 firewood, 1 food; agent_2 gets 1 water, 2 firewood, 1 food.
No clear mutual acceptance by both agents; agent_2 repeatedly proposes 'Let's finalize' but agent_1 never explicitly accepts.
Both agents confirm a 'final deal' but their described allocations conflict (Turn 9 vs Turn 10), so no clear agreed allocation can be determined.

Agents explicitly agreed (Turns 10-12) that agent_1 takes 2 firewood and 1 food and agent_2 takes 1 firewood and 2 food. Water was not specified; inferred a 1/2 split (agent_1/agent_2) given agent_2 has more children.

Agent 2 explicitly finalizes the split in Turn 12 (and reaffirms in Turn 14): agent_1 gets 2 firewood and 2 food; agent_2 takes 1 firewood, 1 food, and all 3 water. There is a minor contradictory phrasing in Turn 13, so confidence is medium.
No explicit acceptance or confirmation of any proposal appears in the final turns; conversation ends with repeated counteroffers.

Although agent_1 states the deal was confirmed (turn 11), the transcript never specifies a clear final allocation (the last proposal in turn 10 is ambiguous about who ends with which quantities), so a definitive allocation cannot be inferred.
Final mutual confirmation occurs in turns 19-20 with agent_1 stating the split (2 food, 2 firewood, 1 water for agent_1) and agent_2 agreeing.

Agent 1 explicitly states final split in turn 11 (3 Food, 2 Firewood for agent_1; 1 Firewood and all 3 Water for agent_2). Agent 2 acknowledges the deal afterward despite an earlier conflicting confirmation, so agreement is inferred with moderate confidence.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 8-10); final confirmation at turn 10. Totals sum to available supplies.
Agents agreed to split water 1-2, initially firewood 1-2; then traded 1 firewood for 1 food (turn 11), yielding these integer allocations consistent with totals. Turn 12 confirms departure.
Turn 13 specifies the allocation (sums to totals); Turn 14 confirms the deal.
Agent 1 proposed a final split in Turn 15 and Agent 2 confirmed the deal in Turn 16.
Turn 16 contains an explicit final allocation and Turn 17 confirms the deal; allocations sum to totals.

Turn 10 explicitly states the accepted split (agent_2 takes 2 water,1 food,1 firewood; agent_1 gets the remainder). Turn 11 confirms agreement. There is slight ambiguity because turn 9 proposed the opposite assignment, so confidence is medium.
Turn 10 contains an explicit confirmed allocation by mturk_agent_2 matching totals.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (first clear agreement at Turn 15), allocating 2 firewood/1 water/1 food to agent_1 and 1 firewood/2 water/2 food to agent_2.
No final mutual agreement was reached; the last turns repeat competing proposals (3F/2Food/1W vs 2F/3Food/1W) with no acceptance.
No explicit final agreement or acceptance appears; transcript ends with repeated counteroffers but no deal.
Both agents repeatedly restated and confirmed the split; final confirmation given in turn 16. Totals match available supplies.
Turn 11 contains the first clear statement of agreement (agent_1: 'I take 1 water, 2 foods, and 2 firewoods'), and subsequent turns confirm the split. Totals match the available packages.
Agent 2 explicitly states the final deal in turn 10: mturk_agent_1 receives 2 firewood, 1 water, and 2 food; remaining packages go to mturk_agent_2.
Both agents explicitly agree to the split (turns 11-13); turn 13 confirms final agreement summing to all 3 packages each resource.
At turn 17 agent_1 proposed 2 water, 2 food, 1 firewood for each; at turn 18 agent_2 explicitly accepted and said 'Let’s confirm this split and move on.'

Although both agents verbally confirmed a split (Turns 13-15), the agreed numbers (Turn 14) sum to more than the available 3 Food packages, making the deal inconsistent with the stated resource totals.
Agents explicitly confirm at turn 16 that mturk_agent_2 takes 2 water, 1 food, 1 firewood and mturk_agent_1 takes the remaining 1 water, 2 food, 2 firewood.
No explicit final acceptance of a specific allocation appears in the transcript; multiple proposals were made but no clear agreed deal.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 12-13; turn 13 contains an explicit 'Deal confirmed' with matching allocation details.

Both agents repeatedly state a split of 2 firewood/1 food for 'me' and 1 firewood/2 food for 'you', but they reverse who 'me' refers to in final confirmations (turns 15–16), producing conflicting assignments and no clear consistent deal.

Turn 4 explicitly finalizes trading 2 Food for 1 Firewood. Allocation assumes agent2 gave 1 Firewood to agent1 and received 2 Food; water distribution was not discussed, so assigned to agent_1 to satisfy totals. Low confidence due to unspecified initial holdings.
No clear mutual agreement — mturk_agent_1 repeatedly proposes and says 'Deal!' at turn 19, but mturk_agent_2 never explicitly accepts or confirms a final split.

Both agents explicitly confirmed a deal in turns 11-12 stating mturk_agent_2 gets 2 firewood, 1 food, 1 water and mturk_agent_1 gets 1 firewood, 1 food, 1 water; transcript contains some internal inconsistencies about totals, so confidence is medium.
No clear final agreement or explicit acceptance appears; agents repeatedly propose conflicting allocations without confirming a deal.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 12-13: agent2 takes 3 water and 1 firewood; agent1 takes 2 food and 1 firewood.
Agent 1 proposed the final split in Turn 15 and Agent 2 confirmed the deal in Turn 16.
Agents appear to agree but each describes the final split with reversed roles (Turn 7 vs Turn 8), creating ambiguity about who gets 2 firewood vs 2 water. No unambiguous final allocation.
Agent 1 explicitly agrees in Turn 17 to take 2 firewood and 1 water while giving Agent 2 1 firewood and 2 food; later turns contain a contradictory restatement but Turn 17 shows clear agreement.
Turn 12 confirms the deal: mturk_agent_2 takes 2 waters and 1 food; mturk_agent_1 gets all firewood. Remaining items distributed to match totals (3 each).

Turn 12 contains an explicit confirmation of the proposed split. There is an inconsistency/ambiguity in the transcript (pronoun swap between Turn 11 and 12 and totals for Water sum to 2 rather than 3), so allocations are inferred from the original proposal (Turn 11) and confirmed in Turn 12 but with low confidence.
No explicit mutual acceptance or final agreed allocation appears in the transcript. Multiple proposals were made but no turn indicates a confirmed deal.
Turn 11 contains an explicit final split statement confirming the agreed allocation; turn 12 acknowledges the deal.
Agent_1 proposed taking 2 firewood and giving agent_2 two food and one water (turn 11); agent_2 confirmed the split and the return (turn 12). Totals sum to available supplies.
Both agents explicitly state and confirm the final split in turns 18-20 (turn 20 confirms the deal).

Agents agreed on 2-1 split of food and water (agent_1 taking 1 food/2 water per Turn 9, agent_2 accepted in Turn 10) and firewood split 1 (agent_1) to 2 (agent_2) in Turn 11; Turn 12 confirms the deal.

Turn 9 finalizes trading 2 food from agent_1 to agent_2 in exchange for agent_2 taking 1 water, leaving agent_1 with 2 water and 1 food. Firewood was not discussed; allocation is inferred to balance totals.
Turn 12 contains an explicit confirmation of the split (repeated in turn 13 as confirmed). Totals sum to available packages.
Agent 2 proposed taking 2 water, 2 food, 1 firewood in turn 10 and Agent 1 explicitly agreed to that allocation in turn 11; subsequent turns repeat the agreement.
Turn 8 contains an explicit confirmed deal specifying the exact split for both agents.
Turn 9 contains an explicit agreed final split matching total supplies (3 each).
Turn 13 explicitly states the final split: agent_1 gets 3 water, 2 firewood; agent_2 gets 3 food and 1 firewood. Earlier turns (10–12) also confirm agreement.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (agent_1 at turn 19, agent_2 confirms leaving at turn 20): agent_1 takes 3 water and 2 firewood; agent_2 takes 1 food and 1 firewood.
Both agents explicitly agree in Turn 9 to the allocation: agent_1 gets 2 food, 2 water, 1 firewood; agent_2 gets 1 food, 1 water, 2 firewood.
No explicit final agreed numeric allocation in the transcript; agent_2 claims confirmation but no concrete division of the 3 packages each was stated.
Both agents explicitly agreed to the split (agent_2 proposed 2/2/2 vs 1/1/1 at turn 10; agent_1 confirmed at turn 11).

No clear mutual agreement: agents repeatedly propose conflicting allocations (agent_1 proposes 2/1 firewood etc.; agent_2 insists on 3 firewood and 0 others). No turn shows both accepting the same deal.

Agents clearly confirmed a deal in turn 7: mturk_agent_1 stated the agreed split (each 1.5 firewood, and water/food as 1/2 and 2/1). Firewood was negotiated as 1.5 each (fractional), so integer allocations above reflect a rounded interpretation; confidence is low due to the fractional firewood agreement.

Both agents explicitly confirm a final split in Turn 9 (each gets 1 firewood; agent_1 gets 3 water; agent_2 gets 3 food). Note: this allocation leaves 1 firewood package unassigned in the transcript, so confidence is reduced.

Although agents verbally confirmed a split (Turns 10-12), the agreed numbers are internally inconsistent with the stated total supplies (water totals exceed 3), so no clear feasible deal can be inferred.
Turn 13 contains mturk_agent_1's explicit agreement to the split proposed (mirrored in turn 12); totals match available packages.
Turn 8 explicitly agrees: 'You get 3 firewoods, I'll take 2 waters and 1 food.' 'You' is mturk_agent_1, leaving remaining items to them.
No explicit mutual agreement: mturk_agent_1 repeatedly proposes a split (e.g., turn 9) but mturk_agent_2 never accepts and continues to counteroffer, so no clear final deal.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split in turns 14-15; turn 15 is the final explicit agreement matching totals.
Turn 10 contains an explicit 'Agreed! Final split' specifying allocations. There is a conflicting proposal in Turn 9 with roles reversed, so confidence is medium.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 13-14; final confirmation stated in turn 14.

Turn 9 contains an explicit agreement stating mturk_agent_2 gets 1 firewood, 2 food, 2 water and mturk_agent_1 gets 2 firewood, 1 food, 1 water. Turn 10 contains a role-swapped confirmation likely a slip, so Turn 9 is taken as the final deal.
Turn 13 explicitly states a deal: agent_2 takes 2 food,1 water,1 firewood; agent_1 takes remaining 1 food,2 waters,2 firewood. Agent_2 confirms in Turn 14.
Agent 1 explicitly accepts the split in Turn 15 (2 water, 2 food, 1 firewood for themselves). Agent 2's subsequent confirmation (Turn 16) swaps pronouns, creating some ambiguity.

Agent 2 repeatedly proposes and then finalizes the split (turn 16): agent1 gets the remaining 1 water and 1 firewood; agent2 keeps 2 water, 2 firewood and takes the 2 remaining food plus their original 1 food, totaling 3 food.
No turn contains a clear mutual acceptance; proposals are exchanged and repeatedly rejected, with agent_2 repeatedly insisting on 3 water.

Agents agreed on a 2-firewood for 1-food trade (turns 6-8) and to split the remaining 1 firewood and 2 food; agent1 accepted this at turn 9. Water was not discussed, so it was split as 1/2 (ambiguous), giving medium confidence.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split: agent_2 gets 1 firewood and 1 food and 2 water; agent_1 gets 2 firewood and 2 food and 1 water (confirmed in turns 9-11).
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split (agent1: 3 food, 1 firewood; agent2: 3 water, 2 firewood) by turn 17.
Agent 2 accepted the proposal in Turn 16 and Agent 1 confirmed the deal in Turn 17; allocations sum to the available totals.
Agent2 offered 2 food + 1 water to Agent1 (turn 8) and Agent1 confirmed the deal (turn 9). Remaining items assigned to Agent2 to match totals.
Turn 13 contains explicit confirmed final split matching total available packages; Turn 14 acknowledges the deal.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (Turn 12 proposed, Turn 13 agreed), totaling 3 of each item.
Turn 14 contains the clear final allocation: agent_2: 2 food, 2 water, 1 firewood; agent_1: 1 food, 1 water, 2 firewood.
No explicit acceptance or final agreement was reached; participants repeated proposals but never confirmed a deal.
Agent 2 proposed taking 2 water and 1 firewood (turn 12) and Agent 1 explicitly confirmed that allocation in turn 13.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split in turn 10: agent_2 gets 2 water, 1 firewood, 1 food; agent_1 gets 1 water, 2 firewood. Remaining food packages were not allocated in the dialogue.
Both agents explicitly state and confirm the same numeric split (turns 8-9) and agent_2 thanks for confirming the deal in turn 10; allocations sum to totals.
Both agents repeatedly confirmed the split: mturk_agent_1 takes 2 firewood and 1 water, mturk_agent_2 takes 1 firewood and 2 water. Food was not discussed.
No explicit acceptance of an offer appears—agents repeatedly propose trades but never confirm a final agreement.

Agent1 agreed to give 3 water and proposed trading 1 food for 1 firewood; Agent2 confirmed the deal in turn 6. Allocation inferred as Agent2: 3 water, 1 firewood, 0 food; Agent1 gets the remaining items.
Turn 7 contains an explicit agreement and final split that sums to the total packages.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split in turns 9 and 10, with totals matching available packages.
Turn 7 contains explicit confirmation of the split matching the allocations that sum to the total packages.
Final explicit agreement stated in Turn 13. There was an earlier conflicting confirmation in Turn 11, so confidence is medium.
Both agents explicitly agree on the split at turns 11–13: agent_2 gets 2 water,1 food,1 firewood; agent_1 gets 1 water,2 food,2 firewood.
Agent 2 explicitly confirms the split at turn 12 and agent 1 acknowledges confirmation at turn 13; allocations sum correctly to totals.
Turn 10 contains an explicit confirmed final split; 'You' refers to mturk_agent_1 who was addressed by mturk_agent_2.
No explicit mutual acceptance of any offer; proposals conflict (agent_1 seeks 2-1 firewood split, agent_2 repeatedly demands 3 firewood).
Agents repeatedly converge on splitting firewood 2-1 with mturk_agent_2 offering 1 food and 1 water to mturk_agent_1 (first clear proposal at turn 6); allocations match totals.
No clear final acceptance or mutually consistent agreed allocation appears in the transcript; participants repeatedly propose offers but never confirm a deal.
No explicit agreed allocation was stated. One agent claimed a deal was confirmed (Turn 6) but no specific allocation or mutual confirmation appears in the transcript.
No explicit acceptance of any final proposal. Agent 2 proposes a final split in turn 20, but agent 1 never agrees, so no clear deal reached.
No explicit final agreement or acceptance was reached; exchanges repeat offers but no deal confirmation.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 12-14: agent_1 takes 1 water and 2 firewood; agent_2 takes 2 water and 2 food.
Turn 9 contains an explicit confirmation of the split (agent_1 confirms the allocation); turn 10 also acknowledges it.
Both agents explicitly confirm a 2-1 split for each resource; agent_1 agrees in turn 9.
No turn contains an explicit acceptance or confirmation of a final deal; participants only repeat competing proposals.
Final confirmation at turn 16 states agent_2 gets 2 firewood, 2 food, 1 water and agent_1 gets 1 firewood, 1 food, 2 water; totals match available supplies.
No turn contains an explicit acceptance of any proposal; multiple proposals were made but no final agreement was confirmed.
Turn 12 explicitly agrees to 2 firewood for 1 food; earlier turns assign 2 waters to agent_1. Totals sum to available packages.
No clear final agreement recorded; the transcript ends with repeated offers and no acceptance.
Both agents repeatedly confirm the split where mturk_agent_1 receives 2 water, 1 food, 1 firewood and mturk_agent_2 receives 2 food, 2 firewood, 1 water (first explicitly confirmed at turn 10).
Agent 1 explicitly accepts the deal in Turn 11: agent1 gets 3 Firewood, 2 Water, 1 Food; agent2 gets 0 Firewood, 1 Water, 2 Food.
No explicit agreement or confirmation of a final allocation; conversation ends with repeated proposals but no acceptance.
No explicit accepted agreement in the transcript—agents made competing offers but never agreed on a final split.
Turn 11 contains an explicit confirmation of the split matching totals; both agents then agree in turn 12.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 14-16), with turn 16 providing the final confirmation.
Explicit confirmation in Turn 8 specifies allocations that sum to totals.
Turn 9 contains an explicit confirmed deal with allocations summing to the available packages.
Final two turns conflict: Turn 11 and Turn 12 present contradictory allocations, so no clear mutually agreed deal can be determined.
Turn 13 contains an explicit agreed final split matching turn 12's confirmation: agent_1 gets 1F/2Food/1W and agent_2 gets 2F/1Food/2W.
No clear final agreement: agents explicitly confirm conflicting allocations (turn 15/17 vs turn 16), so deal is ambiguous.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same split (turns 17-18); turn 17 is the first clear confirmation. Totals match available supplies.

Final explicit confirmation in Turn 14 states agent_2 gets 2 Food and 1 Water and agent_1 gets 1 Firewood; remaining packages allocated to balance totals (sums to 3 each). Some earlier turns contained conflicting statements, so confidence is medium.
Agent 1 takes all 3 water and gives 1 food to agent 2; firewood split 2-1 in agent 2's favor was agreed and explicitly confirmed by agent 2.

Turns 10–11 show agreement that mturk_agent_2 receives 2 firewood and 1 water in exchange for 1 food (so mturk_agent_1 ends up with 1 food). Remaining items allocated to balance totals. Turn 12 contains a contradictory phrasing, lowering confidence.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (agent_1 at turn 15; agent_2 previously agreed at turns 14 and 16), matching total available items.

Both agents agreed to 2 food and 2 water for agent_1 and 1 food and 1 water for agent_2 (turns 6-8). They stated 1.5 firewood each; packages are indivisible so I assigned remaining firewood as 1 and 2 (assignment arbitrary).
Both agents explicitly confirm that agent_2 gets 2 water and 1 food, and agent_1 gets 2 food and 1 firewood; remaining items allocated to satisfy totals.

Participants repeatedly confirm allocations but statements are internally inconsistent with the total supplies (claims to split 'remaining' items lead to impossible totals), so no clear valid integer allocation can be inferred.
Both agents explicitly confirm a final allocation in turn 13, though the allocations (2+2 firewood = 4) conflict with the stated total supplies (3 each), so confidence is low.
Both agents explicitly agree to the split in Turns 10-11: agent_2 accepts the proposed allocation in Turn 10 and agent_1 confirms in Turn 11.
Both agents explicitly agree to the split (turns 10-11); allocations sum to available packages.

Turn 12 explicitly states agent_2 will take 3 firewood, 2 water, and 1 food, and agent_1 gets the remaining 1 food and 1 water. However this allocation is inconsistent with the stated total of 3 food packages (only 2 foods are allocated), so confidence is low.
Both agents explicitly restated and accepted the same allocation in turns 19-20: agent_1 gets 2 food and 1 water; agent_2 gets 1 firewood and 2 water.
Agent 2 explicitly offered 3 firewood and 1 water to agent 1 in exchange for 2 water and 1 food (turn 10), and agent 1 confirmed the deal (turn 11). Totals sum to available supplies.
Turn 12 explicitly confirms the final split: agent_1 receives 3 firewood, agent_2 receives 2 water and 1 food; remaining packages allocated to the other agent to total 3 each.

Both agents explicitly confirm the split in turns 12–13. The agreed numbers (3 food for agent_2 and 2 food for agent_1) conflict with the stated total inventory (3 food total), so confidence is reduced.
Both agents explicitly agree (turns 17 and 19) to the split: agent_2 gets 2 food, 2 firewood, 1 water; agent_1 gets 1 food, 1 firewood, 2 water.
No clear final agreement was reached; the transcript contains repeated proposals but no acceptance.

Both agents explicitly confirm the split at Turn 13: agent_1 takes 3 firewood, agent_2 gets 2 food, and they state they both have 1 water. The remaining 1 water package is not mentioned, so allocation of that package is ambiguous.
Both agents explicitly confirm the allocation (first explicitly stated in Turn 6, final confirmation in Turn 9): agent1 gets all 3 Food, agent2 gets all 3 Firewood and 3 Water.
No turn shows mutual acceptance. Agents repeatedly propose conflicting final splits; no clear agreed deal is reached.
Agent 1 explicitly states the final split in Turn 13 and Agent 2 confirms the deal in Turn 14. Totals match the available supplies.
Agents explicitly confirm a final split where agent_1 gets 2 firewood and 1 food and agent_2 gets 1 water; remaining items inferred to satisfy totals (3 each).
Turn 9 contains an explicit confirmation of the final split totaling the available packages.
Turn 13 contains a clear mutual confirmation: mturk_agent_1 states they get 1 firewood, 2 water, 2 food and the other gets 2 firewood, 1 water, 1 food, matching the agreed split.
Turn 12 explicitly confirms the final split: agent_2 gets 1 Food, 2 Water, 1 Firewood; agent_1 gets the remaining 2 Food, 1 Water, 2 Firewood.
Turn 19 contains a clear complete proposal allocating all 3 of each resource (2W/2F/1FW to agent_2; 1W/1F/2FW to agent_1). Agent_2's subsequent turn repeats the proposal, implying acceptance.
No explicit acceptance or final agreement was reached; agents kept proposing different allocations through the last turn.

No clear mutual agreement: mturk_agent_1 repeatedly proposes a final split (e.g., turns 3,5,9,11, etc.), but mturk_agent_2 never accepts and repeatedly offers a different proposal; therefore no confirmed deal.
No explicit acceptance of any specific proposal; repeated proposals from agent_2 but agent_1 never agrees, so no clear final deal.
No final acceptance or explicit agreement appears in the transcript. Agents exchanged and repeated proposals but never confirmed a deal.

Final explicit confirmation in turn 20 states the split (agent_2: 1 water,2 food,2 firewood; agent_1: 2 water,1 food,1 firewood). There was an earlier contradictory confirmation, so confidence is medium.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 11-13): one agent receives 2 firewood, 1 water, 1 food while the other receives 1 firewood, 2 water, 2 food; confirmed in turn 13.
No explicit mutual acceptance recorded. Agent 2 repeatedly proposes a final split, but Agent 1 never accepts or confirms agreement.
Multiple confirmations culminate in Turn 15 where mturk_agent_1 explicitly confirms the final split (you=mturk_agent_2: 2 water,1 food,2 firewood; I=mturk_agent_1: 1 water,2 food,1 firewood).
Turn 14 contains explicit agreement stating the final split; totals match available packages.
Both agents explicitly agree to the split in Turn 19 (confirmed again in Turn 20): agent_1 gets 2 water, 1 food, 2 firewood; agent_2 gets 1 water, 2 food, 1 firewood.
Agents explicitly agree on the split in turns 12-14: agent_1 gets 2 water and 1 food (0 firewood); agent_2 takes 1 water, 2 food, and all 3 firewood.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (agent_1 agrees at turn 9; agents 2 and 1 reconfirm in turns 10-12). Totals match available supplies.
Both agents explicitly confirm that agent_2 receives 3 firewood and 2 water while agent_1 receives 2 food (final confirmation at turn 8). Remaining 1 water and 1 food were not discussed.
No clear mutual agreement or explicit acceptance; agents repeatedly propose different final splits (agent_1 insists on 3 water/1 food for self, agent_2 proposes a different split) so no deal reached.
Turn 8 explicitly confirms the split: agent1 gets 1 food, 2 firewood, 1 water; agent2 gets 2 food, 1 firewood, 2 water; counts sum to totals.

Agent 1 repeatedly finalizes a split where they take 2 food and 2 firewood and give agent 2 one of each; agent 2 never objects and earlier accepted a 2-for-2 swap. Agent 2 stated they have plenty of water, so remaining 3 water assumed with agent 2.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 9-10) allocating 1 food/2 firewood/1 water to agent_1 and 2 food/1 firewood/2 water to agent_2.
Agents explicitly agreed in turns 8–9: agent_2 accepts in turn 8 and agent_1 confirms the deal in turn 9.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (turns 8-10), with mturk_agent_2 taking 2 food & 1 water and mturk_agent_1 taking 2 firewood & 1 water.
Both agents explicitly confirm the final split at turns 10-12; turn 12 states the agreement clearly with allocations that sum to the total available.

Although both agents verbally confirmed a split (Turns 11-12), the confirmed allocations (agent_1: 2 firewood + 2 water; agent_2: 2 firewood + 3 water) exceed the available totals (3 waters total), making the final deal inconsistent and not a clear valid agreement.
Turn 13 explicitly states the final split (2 food,2 firewood,1 water for agent_1; 1 food,1 firewood,2 water for agent_2); turn 14 confirms the deal.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (first clearly stated by agent_2 in turn 12 and reiterated in turns 14-16). Totals match 3 of each item.
Both agents explicitly agreed to the split (turns 12-13); turn 13 provides final confirmation.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same final split in turns 14-16, with agent_2 stating 'you get 2 food, 2 firewood, and 1 water' (you = agent_1) and agent_1 agreeing.

Agents explicitly agreed (turns 9-11) to 2 food and 2 firewood for agent_1 and 1 food and 1 firewood for agent_2; water was discussed as to be split earlier but not restated, so inferred as 2-1 to match the food split.
Both agents explicitly confirmed the split (turns 16-18): allocations sum to totals (3 each).
No explicit final agreement or acceptance from mturk_agent_2—agents repeatedly propose allocations but never confirm a deal.
Turn 10 explicitly confirms the deal: agent_2 accepts 2 food and 1 firewood in exchange for 2 water; totals sum to 3 each.
No clear final deal: agents give conflicting confirmed allocations in turns 12 and 13 (each claims the 2F/2W/1Food bundle for themselves), so a single unambiguous allocation cannot be determined.

Agents explicitly agreed to trade one water for one firewood (turns 7-8) and to split the remaining supplies (turn 10). Transcript states a 2-water/3-food remainder and a 1.5 food each split (fractional), so food division is ambiguous; I allocated integers summing to totals (gave agent_1 two food, agent_2 one) to satisfy package counts.
Turn 10 contains an explicit confirmation of the final split: agent_2 gets 2 Firewood and 1 Food; agent_1 gets 1 Water, 1 Firewood, and 2 Food.

Final explicit confirmation at turn 16 states agent_2 takes 2 waters,1 firewood,1 food and agent_1 takes 1 water,2 firewoods,1 food. Totals do not match the stated available 3 food packages, so confidence is medium.
Turn 12 (mturk_agent_2) explicitly states the final split, and turn 13 (mturk_agent_1) confirms the deal.
Both agents explicitly confirm a final split at turn 17: agent_1 gets 2 waters, 2 foods, 1.5 firewood; agent_2 gets 1 water, 1 food, 1.5 firewood. Firewood is split fractionally in the transcript.

Agents explicitly agreed that agent_1 takes all 3 water (turns 9-11) and agent_2 takes 2 food and 1 firewood (turns 9-11). The remaining 2 firewood and 1 food were not explicitly assigned; to satisfy the total counts they are provisionally given to agent_1. Confidence is low due to this implicit assignment.

Although there was an agreement at turn 16, subsequent turns (17-20) show both agents proposing different final splits with no clear mutual acceptance of the same allocation. Therefore no clear final deal is reached.
Both agents repeatedly propose and restate the same split (agent1: 2 food, 2 firewood, 1 water; agent2: 1 food, 1 firewood, 2 water). Agent1 explicitly declares taking 2/2/1 at turn 11.
Agents agreed to trade 3 firewood for 1 water and 1 food (turn 9), and agent_1 confirmed the deal (turn 11). Totals adjusted to the 3-package limits.
Both agents explicitly confirm the split (agent_1 in turn 9 and agent_2 finalizing in turn 10). Allocations are inconsistent with total supplies but reflect the agreed claims.
Both agents explicitly confirmed the final split in turns 11-12; turn 12 contains an explicit 'Agreed. Final split' statement.
Both agents explicitly confirm the same split in turns 11-13, with turn 13 giving the final confirmation.
